name: Camino Sanabrés and Cernadilla Reservoir Loop
name_original: Mombuey, Embalse de Cernadilla y río Negro — Circular en la Comarca de Sanabria
description: This 32.1-mile touring cycling loop combines sections of the Camino Sanabrés with views of Cernadilla Reservoir, rated difficult.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mombuey traverse a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including quiet country roads and sections of the historic Camino Sanabrés. The region features significant natural elements such as the Cernadilla Reservoir and the Valparaíso reservoir, offering scenic views. Pine forests and open countryside define many of the cycling paths, providing a mix of environments for riders. Best touring cycling routes around Mombuey The most popular touring cycling route is Camino Sanabrés and Cernadilla Reservoir Loop, a 32.1 miles (51.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route combines a section of the Camino Sanabrés with views of the Cernadilla Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puebla de Sanabria Castle – Old Town of Puebla de Sanabria loop from Asturianos, a difficult 36.7 miles (59.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and passes by historical sites. Local touring cyclists also love the Villardeciervos Village loop from Villardeciervos, a 17.6 miles (28.4 km) trail leading through varied countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Touring cycling around Mombuey is defined by sections of the Camino Sanabrés, scenic reservoirs, and rural country roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Mombuey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 30 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mombuey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mombuey?
A: There are 10 touring cycling routes available around Mombuey, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes range from moderate to difficult, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mombuey?
A: The region experiences varied weather, but generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for touring cycling. Summer can be warm, but routes that pass through pine forests or near reservoirs like the Cernadilla Reservoir or Embalse de Valparaíso can provide cooler sections.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Mombuey?
A: While many routes around Mombuey are rated as difficult due to elevation changes, the area does offer moderate options. For a less strenuous ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24549491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Valparaíso loop from Rionegro del Puente&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated moderate and offers scenic views of the reservoir.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Mombuey?
A: Mombuey's natural landscapes, including pine forests and open countryside, are generally welcoming for dogs. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near wildlife or on sections of the Camino Sanabrés where you might encounter other pilgrims and cyclists. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Mombuey's touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain around Mombuey is diverse, featuring quiet country roads, sections of the historic Camino Sanabrés, and paths through pine forests. You'll encounter varied landscapes, from the open countryside to areas with views of reservoirs like the Cernadilla Reservoir and Embalse de Valparaíso. Expect some elevation changes, as many routes are rated difficult.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Mombuey area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Mombuey are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1402714548/camino-sanabres-and-cernadilla-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés and Cernadilla Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route that combines historical paths with scenic reservoir views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villardeciervos Village loop from Villardeciervos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Mombuey offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can visit the impressive 13th-century Templar tower of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5457926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María de Mombuey&lt;/a&gt;. Routes often pass by scenic spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Valparaíso&lt;/a&gt; on the Embalse de Valparaíso, and you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3941370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Pilgrim of Rionegro del Puente&lt;/a&gt; along the Camino Sanabrés.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Mombuey?
A: Parking is generally available in Mombuey village and other starting points for routes, such as Asturianos or Villardeciervos. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24310778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Picnic Area – Muelas de los Caballeros loop from El Empalme&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find parking near designated picnic areas or trailheads.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access cycling routes?
A: While Mombuey is a rural area, it is situated along the historic Ruta de la Plata, which has some bus services connecting larger towns. However, public transport options directly to trailheads with bike carriage facilities might be limited. It's best to check local bus schedules and policies regarding bicycles in advance if you plan to use public transport.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Mombuey?
A: The touring cycling routes around Mombuey are predominantly rated as difficult, with 6 out of 10 routes falling into this category. These routes often feature significant elevation changes. There are also 4 moderate routes available, providing options for those seeking a less challenging ride.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Mombuey?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mombuey are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the integration with the historic Camino Sanabrés, and the scenic views of reservoirs and pine forests.

Q: Are there any less crowded touring cycling routes in Mombuey?
A: While the Camino Sanabrés sections can see more activity, especially during pilgrimage seasons, many of the country roads and forest trails offer a quieter experience. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24310778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Picnic Area – Muelas de los Caballeros loop from El Empalme&lt;/a&gt; often provide a more secluded ride through the natural landscape.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on Mombuey's cycling routes?
A: The natural environment around Mombuey, including pine forests and areas near reservoirs, is home to various wildlife. You might spot local bird species, deer, or other small mammals. The region is also close to the Iberian Wolf Center, highlighting the presence of significant wildlife in the broader area.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The Camino Sanabrés and Cernadilla Reservoir Loop is approximately 51.7 km long and typically takes around 3 hours and 56 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. This duration can vary based on your cycling speed, stops, and the specific conditions of the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain, characteristic of the broader Camino Sanabrés. You can expect sections that traverse plains, transition into scrubland, and include undulating countryside. While the specific loop around Cernadilla Reservoir might have its own characteristics, the general area can feature forest tracks and some off-road sections, making it suitable for touring bicycles capable of handling diverse surfaces.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as difficult. The Camino Sanabrés itself is often recommended for experienced cyclists due to its varied terrain and ascents. While the Cernadilla Reservoir section might have easier parts, the overall difficulty suggests it's best suited for cyclists with some experience and good physical condition.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Sanabria region, including this loop, is generally during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). These seasons offer milder temperatures and more pleasant conditions for cycling. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder weather and potentially more challenging conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: While the Camino Sanabrés generally allows dogs, it's important to keep them under control, preferably on a leash, especially in rural areas, near livestock, or on shared paths. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them. Be mindful of the route's length and difficulty when bringing a dog.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Mombuey. You can typically find parking in or near the village center to begin your ride. As the Cernadilla Reservoir area is less developed for specific touring cycling loops, parking options might be more informal, so plan to park responsibly in designated areas or where permitted.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Camino Sanabrés and Cernadilla Reservoir Loop. It is a public route, and access is generally free. However, if you plan to stay in private accommodations or visit specific attractions, those may have their own costs.

faqs.question: What interesting sights can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy several notable sights. These include a beautiful View of Embalse de Cernadilla, sections of the Arroyo del Regata Trail, and a scenic Path Along the Río Negro. The starting point in Mombuey also features the historic Church of Santa María de Mombuey.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. Approximately 29% of the loop follows parts of the Camino Sanabrés (East) and the Mtb Camino Sanabrés (Este) / Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Leste). Smaller segments also align with the Cno. de Entrepeñas a San Salvador and the Camino Muelas de los Caballeros - Donadillo.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: While the Camino Sanabrés is known for having limited services compared to more popular routes, Mombuey, the starting point, is a village where you might find basic amenities. For more options, towns like Puebla de Sanabria, a notable stop on the broader Camino Sanabrés, offer more extensive services including accommodations, restaurants, and grocery stores. It's advisable to plan ahead for provisions and overnight stays, especially if you venture into more remote sections.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this touring cycling loop?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and varied terrain, pack essentials for touring cycling. This includes appropriate cycling gear, a repair kit for your bike, sufficient water and snacks, sun protection, and layers of clothing for changing weather. Due to potentially limited services, carrying extra provisions is a good idea. A map or GPS device is also recommended for navigation.
